steps.title: Identify the antecedent
steps.content: The blank possesses narrative, and the thing that has the narrative is the singular epic poem Raghuvaṃśa. So we need a singular possessive pronoun with no apostrophe.

steps.title: Apply the rule
steps.content:
"Its" is the singular possessive (the poem's narrative). "It's" means "it is" (a contraction). "Their" and "they're" are plural — wrong, since the poem is singular.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
